name: Telegram
description: Connect AzulClaw to Telegram through the Azure Bot relay pattern.

serviceBusConnectionString.title: Service Bus connection string
serviceBusConnectionString.description: Connection string shared by the Azure Function relay and this local runtime.

serviceBusInboundQueue.title: Inbound queue
serviceBusInboundQueue.description: Queue where the Azure Function relay writes Bot Framework activities.

serviceBusOutboundQueue.title: Outbound queue
serviceBusOutboundQueue.description: Queue where the local runtime writes correlated replies for the relay.

serviceBusUseSessions.title: Use Service Bus sessions
serviceBusUseSessions.description: Use auto for normal operation. Set false only for channels that do not require synchronous replies.

botSyncReplyTimeoutSeconds.title: Synchronous reply timeout
botSyncReplyTimeoutSeconds.description: Seconds the relay waits for a correlated reply before returning a fallback response.

microsoftAppId.title: Microsoft App ID
microsoftAppId.description: Azure Bot application ID used by Bot Framework auth.

microsoftAppPassword.title: Microsoft App password
microsoftAppPassword.description: Azure Bot application password used by Bot Framework auth.

microsoftAppTenantId.title: Microsoft App tenant ID
microsoftAppTenantId.description: Optional tenant ID for single-tenant Bot Framework auth.

botRelayRequireAuth.title: Require Bot Framework auth in relay
botRelayRequireAuth.description: Keep enabled for production relay deployments.

allowedUserIds.title: Allowed Telegram user IDs
allowedUserIds.description: Comma-separated Telegram user IDs. Leave empty to allow all users that can reach the bot.

allowedChatIds.title: Allowed Telegram chat IDs
allowedChatIds.description: Comma-separated Telegram chat IDs. Leave empty to allow all chats that can reach the bot.

secrets.name: SERVICE_BUS_CONNECTION_STRING
secrets.title: Service Bus connection string
secrets.description: Connection string for the relay queues.

capabilities.description: Receive and send Telegram messages through the Azure relay.
